violations.text: Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Per the PIC, decluttering of the basement area is to continue.) New Violation. Correct By: 05/19/2025

The following areas of the physical facilities were observed with an accumulation of soil residue, food debris, and old droppings... - Floor under hibachi grills (cleaning daily) - Floor under/behind equipment in kitchen and bar area (Clean daily) - Floor along walls behind equipment in the basement The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. (Cleaning company currently scheduled for biweekly cleaning. PIC is to ensure facility is cleaned daily to aid in cleaning and pest control programs.) New Violation. Correct By: 05/19/2025

The presence of mice is not controlled as evidenced by trapped live and dead mice observed in the upstairs dining and bar areas. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Currently, establishment is regularly treated for pest control biweekly. Establishment is to increase pest control to weekly until pest issue is under control. New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2025

At the time of inspection, dead and trapped mice were observed on glue boards. Dead or trapped pests shall be removed from control devices and the PREMISES at a frequency that prevents their accumulation, decomposition, or the attraction of pests. Dead and trapped mice were disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Per the PIC, decluttering of the basement area is to continue.) New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2025

The following areas of the physical facilities were observed with an accumulation of soil residue, food debris, and droppings... - Floor under hibachi grills (cleaning daily) - Floor under/behind equipment in kitchen area (Clean daily) - Floor along walls behind equipment in the basement The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. (Cleaning company currently scheduled for biweekly cleaning. PIC is to ensure facility is cleaned daily to aid in cleaning and pest control programs.) Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets to the 2-door reach-in cooler were observed in poor repair. Equipment is to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Per the PIC, decluttering of the basement area is to continue.) New Violation. Correct By: 11/25/2025

Old droppings were observed under and behind equipment in various areas. PIC is to ensure all areas are thoroughly cleaned to aid in the current pest control program. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2025

Raw animal foods (beef) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods in the 2-door reach-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Food items were rearranged for proper storage at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked by marking with an asterisk. Food items on the menu that are possibly undercooked per order are to be marked with an asterisk alerting to the consumer advisory provided. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Reduced Oxygen Packaged Swordfish was observed thawing in its intact packaging. REDUCED OXYGEN PACKAGED FISH that bears a label indicating that it is to be kept frozen until time of use shall be removed from the reduced oxygen environment prior to its thawing under refrigeration. Based on temperature of ROP Fish at time of inspection (34 degrees Fahrenheit), fish was removed from packaging for proper thawing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets to the 2-door reach-in cooler were observed in poor repair. Equipment is to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

The non-food-contact surfaces of the hibachi grills (along sides/edges of flat tops) were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food-contact surfaces of equipment are to be cleaned at a frequency necessary to preclude the accumulation of soil residues. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

The pre-flush spray hose at the dishwasher area is hanging below the flood rim. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Per the PIC, decluttering of the basement area is to continue.) Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/25/2025

An employee was observed filling a glass of water for a customer at the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025

At the time of inspection, packaged loaves of bread ion the basement area were observed with rodent gnawing holes in the packaging. Loaves of bread were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025

Raw animal foods (beef) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (sauces) in the kitchen reach-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Foods were rearranged for proper storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwashers in the kitchen and the bar as evidenced by the failure of the thermo-label (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black, and the thermometer plate used. Per the PIC, staff will use the 3 bay sinks at the kitchen and bar areas for proper sanitizing with use of quaternary ammonium tablets. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025

The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked by marking with an asterisk. Food items on the menu that are possibly undercooked per customer order are to be marked with an asterisk alerting the consumer advisory provided. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Rodent droppings (old and new) were observed in areas of the bar, kitchen, and basement, as well as gnawed food product in the basement. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. (Current pest control is biweekly.) New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025
At the time of inspection, a ceiling hatch was observed open exposing the plates, utensils, and ice bin below to possible contamination from the premises. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025

The following equipment was observed in need of repair... - Gaskets to the kitchen 2 door reach-in cooler were observed torn - A broken/chipped ladle was observed in use (ladle was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection). - A knife was observed with a chipped blade Equipment is to be maintained in good repair or disposed of if no longer repairable. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025
The facility is lacking thermo-labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine at the kitchen and bar high temp. dish machines. New Violation. Correct By: 02/03/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing tablets for the 3 aby sinks. New Violation. Correct By: 02/03/2025

The non-food-contact surfaces of the following were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris... - Refrigeration surfaces - Cookline surfaces - Hibachi grill surfaces (side walls and below shelving) Non-FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to preclude accumulation of soil residues. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

The pre-flush spray hose at the dish machine is hanging below the flood rim. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

The following areas of the physical facilities were observed with an accumulation of soil residue, food debris, and droppings... - Floor under hibachi grills- Floor under/behind equipment in kitchen area- Floor along walls behind equipment in the basement- Floor of the walk-in coolerThe physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. (Per the PIC, continued decluttering is to occur.) New Violation. Correct By: 11/25/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/25/2024

The floor in the basement of the facility was observed to have an accumulation of old droppings and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the current pest control program. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/19/2024

Three dead or trapped mice were observed in the back basement area behind office. Dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests shall be removed from control devices and the PREMISES at a frequency that prevents their accumulation, decomposition, or the attraction of pests. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/29/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/25/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/25/2024
Several blocks of rodenticide were observed being used in the dining area. Rodent bait shall be contained in a covered, tamper-resistant bait station. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2024

Dead mice were observed in basement storage area. Dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests shall be removed from control devices and the PREMISES at a frequency that prevents their accumulation, decomposition, or the attraction of pests. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by a large accumulation of rodent droppings observed throughout the kitchen, bar, dining, and basement areas. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/25/2024

Three dead mice and one large rodent were observed in the basement area and outside of building respectively. Dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests shall be removed from control devices and the PREMISES at a frequency that prevents their accumulation, decomposition, or the attraction of pests. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by live rodent activity noted during inspection and a large accumulation of rodent droppings observed throughout the dining, bar, kitchen, and basement areas. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 11/25/2024

At the time of inspection, the floors in the basement area, under/behind equipment in the bar area, and in the closet near the exit were observed to have soil residue, debris, and old droppings and 2 dead rodents. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the current pest control program. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area and closet near exit door. Per the PIC and as per observation of inspector, some unnecessary/non-functional equipment was observed to be removed from the establishment since previous inspection. Per the PIC, cleanout of the basement area is to continue, new dumpster needed. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions and to aid in the current pest control program. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2024

At the time of inspection, possible access points for rodent entrance were observed in the basement, bar area, and the closet near the exit. PIC to continue to monitor, search for, and seal any possible rodent entry points in the basement, basement office, and the bar area. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Per the PIC and as per observation of inspector, some unnecessary/non-functional equipment was observed to be removed from the establishment since previous inspection. Per the PIC, cleanout of the basement area is to continue, new dumpster needed. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions and to aid in the current pest control program. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024

At the time of inspection, the floors in the basement area and under/behind equipment in the bar area were observed to have an accumulation of soil residue, debris, and droppings. Per the PIC and inspector observation, cleaning since previous inspection has occurred and is to continue, The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the monitoring of the current pest control program. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024

At the time of inspection, a bag of sugar in basement storage was observed to be adulterated by gnawing. The bag of sugar was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024

At the time of inspection, 2 dead mice were observed in the basement area. Dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests shall be removed from control devices and the PREMISES at a frequency that prevents their accumulation, decomposition, or the attraction of pests. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Per the PIC and as per observation of inspector, some unnecessary/non-functional equipment was observed to be removed from the establishment since previous inspection. Per the PIC, cleanout of the basement area is to continue, new dumpster needed. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions and to aid in the current pest control program. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/01/2024

At the time of inspection, the floor in the basement area were observed to have an accumulation of soil residue, debris, and droppings. Per the PIC and inspector observation, cleaning since previous inspection has occurred and is to continue, The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the monitoring of the current pest control program. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024

At the time of inspection, a bag of sugar and bags of rice were observed to be adulterated by rodent gnawing. Packages of sugar and rice were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/14/2024

Noodles cooled previous day were observed in the walk-in cooler held at 44-45 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Noodles were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/14/2024

The presence of pests is not controlled as evidenced by droppings and gnawed food items in the basement area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Per the PIC, pest control currently inspects/treats 1x monthly. Per the PIC, pest control schedule to be increased. Pest control contacted at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 02/14/2024

The bulkhead door to the basement is not tight-fitting. Since previous inspection, weather stripping was added to the bulkhead door (Openings still observed). Per the PIC, more work is to be done to close any gaps. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/14/2024

At the time of inspection, the basement 2 door cooler was observed to be in need of repair. The middle support between the doors was observed to be loose/unstable preventing the doors from fully sealing when closed. Equipment is to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/01/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions and to aid in the current pest control program. New Violation. Correct By: 03/01/2024

The floor in the basement area were observed to have an accumulation of soil residue, debris, and droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the monitoring of the current pest control program. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/14/2024

At the time of inspection, rice and noodles were observed being held outside of refrigeration using time as a public health control after cooking for use at the Hibachi Tables. PIC is to contact RIDOH CFP Supervisor with written procedures for holding rice and noodles out of refrigeration using time as a public health control. Procedures for proper documentation were discussed with the PIC at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 02/01/2024

Chemical spray bottle(s) were stored with food, equipment, utensils, or single service items. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Spray bottles were moved to protected location at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/01/2024

Dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests shall be removed from control devices and the PREMISES at a frequency that prevents their accumulation, decomposition, or the attraction of pests. Dead Pest removed at the time of insepction. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/01/2024

The bulkhead door to the outside, located in the basement area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Per the PIC, maintenance person to be called for increased weather stripping and to block any gaps in the door when closed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/15/2024

At the time of inspection, clean utensils were stored in containers observed to have dust and debris. Utensils intended for use with food are to be stored in clean/sanitized containers between uses. (Per the PIC, all utensils to be properly cleaned/sanitized prior to use.) New Violation. Correct By: 02/01/2024

The basement 2 door reach-in cooler was observed to be in need of repair. The doors were observed to not shut properly due to a loose middle support between the doors when closed. Equipment is to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/01/2024
The facility is lacking thermo-labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machines at the bar and kitchen areas. New Violation. Correct By: 02/08/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of cookline equipment were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to preclude accumulation of soil residues. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions and to aid in the current pest control program. New Violation. Correct By: 03/01/2024

The floor in the basement area were observed to have an accumulation of soil residue, debris, and droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the monitoring of the current pest control program. New Violation. Correct By: 02/08/2024
